tender.title: Cambridge City Council - Sheltered Housing Carpet Cleaning
tender.description:
Cambridge City Council has 9 indoor Sheltered Housing schemes spread across the city, which providing housing and support to older people. Sheltered housing, is predominantly, for people aged over 60 who wish to carry on living independently but who require some support in order to do so. Our vision is to maximise opportunities for older people to live self-sufficiently by providing good quality accommodation. To provide support in line with individuals needs and to offer activities and social events helping prevent social isolation and to encourage active ageing.

All of the flats within the schemes are either one or two bedrooms, with their own kitchen, bathroom and living areas. The flats are all contained under one roof and have communal facilities such as a communal lounge, with individual flats leading out onto internal corridors.

The communal areas in each scheme are mostly carpeted. This contract is to provide the cleaning of the communal carpets in each scheme, on a yearly basis. Full details of each scheme and it's location can be found below in section 4.3.

address.streetAddress: Unit 5 Greenham Park, Common Road, Witchford, ELY, Cambridgeshire, CB6 2HF

tender.title: SOL - Housing Related Support for People with Disabilities
tender.description:
Service overview:

1.	Enable a person to move to more independent housing, prevent a person being at risk of losing their home, preventing a move to less independent care, if this housing related support was not provided. A primary purpose of the service is to enable the person to live in more independent accommodation than they otherwise might, or to prevent the loss of this independent accommodation.
2.	Focus on developing sustainable independent living skills, over agreed timescales based on individual needs. The long-term development of more independent living skills, or the maintenance of skills which are critical to sustaining the independent living arrangement.
3.	Are provided to people with disabilities, in need of support services, who are at risk of losing their home due to an inability to cope with the demands and requirements of living independently (in a tenancy, as an owner-occupier, or as a lodger or other semi-independent arrangement).
4.	Support people to build a support network to be able to maintain their long- term independence without paid support.
5.	Support approximately 70 people at any one time with their housing related support needs.

tender.title: Housing Related Support Services (HRS) within the Hull City boundary
tender.description:
Hull City Council (HCC) are now seeking tender submissions from providers who are able to provide HRS for one of (up to) 5 sub-lots.  Providers are advised to firstly read the "covering letter" within the attached tender documentation suite for further detail of the breakdown relating to each sub-lot.   There are 3 lots in total which comprise Lot A which is "Adults" (and contains 3 sub-lots). Lot B which is "Young People" which comprise 2 sub-lots. Finally, Lot C which is "Families" which comprise 2 sub-lots. 

Please note this opportunity for all providers will be inclusive of a set rate of £19 per hour, regardless of which sub-lot (or sub-lots), this tender will be evaluated on quality alone. Precise values of the contract are therefore unclear.

tender.title: Together Housing Association Extra Care Service
tender.description: Extra Care Housing is a form of housing with care and support that allows people generally aged 55 and over to live independently within a supportive setting. It includes care and support that is person centred and flexible, so the support is varied dependent on need and it can also be flexed to increase or decrease support so that an individual is appropriately supported. In some instances, this could involve changing the level of care and support available daily. The flexibility can help prevent both hospital and/ or residential care admissions and can also support someone to be effectively discharged from hospital. Residents are individually assessed and may be asked to contribute towards the service. The amount an individual pays depends on available income and capital.

tender.title: Electrical Installation Condition Reports (EICR) for Social Housing 2022-25
tender.description:
Electrical Inspection Condition Reports and Remedial Works to all domestic assets to meet 5 year requirements. 1/5th of housing stock to be inspected each year.

1. To carry out Electrical Inspection Condition Reports for all properties.
2. To carry out C1 & C2 remedial works.
3. Priority rewiring of properties as agreed and approved by F&HDC's Compliance Specialist.

Contract awarded via a further competition under South East Consortium's Internal And External Building Works framework 
Lot 5 (OJEU 2019/S 078-184728)

tender.title: AT1116 Universal Floating Support Service for Vulnerable People within Housing
tender.description:
Full tender documents available on ProContract e-tendering portal.
The objective of the contract support is to ensure issues such as tenancy sustainment, budgeting and life skills are supported to enable the Service User to remain accommodated and/or avoid admission to hospital and consequent loss of their accommodation.
Luton is committed to supporting citizens and vulnerable people to maintain their homes and their tenure, regardless of tenure type. This commitment is the main driver behind the commissioning of the Universal Floating Support Service that also supports the wellbeing and prevention and community safety agendas as well as complimenting the CARE ACT 2014.
